vocabulary.meaning: at a higher level or position than something
further forward in space or time
vocabulary.example: There is a long road ahead.

vocabulary.meaning: a quantity of something
vocabulary.example: A large amount of work is done.

vocabulary.meaning: something accepted as true or real
the middle point of something
vocabulary.example: She stood at the center of the stage.

vocabulary.meaning: happening often; shared by many
the amount paid for something; to have a price
vocabulary.example: What is the cost of this book?

vocabulary.meaning: to show or explain how something works
vocabulary.exampleBn: সে রান্না করার পদ্ধতি প্রদর্শন করল।

vocabulary.meaning: not the same as another
information that proves something is true
vocabulary.example: There is clear evidence of his work.

vocabulary.meaning: the quality of being truthful and fair
a phrase whose meaning differs from its individual words
vocabulary.example: Break a leg is a common idiom.

vocabulary.meaning: not relying on others; free from control
the inner part of something
to become very skilled at something; an expert
vocabulary.example: He mastered the guitar in a year.
vocabulary.exampleBn: সে এক বছরে গিটারে দক্ষ হয়ে গেল।

vocabulary.meaning: the ability to remember; a past experience
right, suitable or correct for a situation
to look at something carefully or quickly
vocabulary.example: Scan the page for key words.
vocabulary.exampleBn: মূল শব্দের জন্য পৃষ্ঠাটি স্ক্যান করো।

vocabulary.meaning: a part or division of something larger
the outside or top layer of something
vocabulary.example: The surface of the table is smooth.

phrases.example: Finish the work ahead of time.
A large amount of rain fell.
She has a strong belief in justice.
The school is in the city center.
Always show honesty in your work.

sentences.en: The birds fly above the clouds.
The amount of work is huge.
The cat is inside the box.

quiz.explanation: Above means at a higher level or position than something.

opts: further forward in space or time

quiz.explanation: Section means a part or division of something larger.

quiz.explanation: Surface means the outside or top layer of something.

story.title: The Memory Inside the Old Book
story.body: Above the city center, on the top floor of the oldest library, there was a section nobody visited. Inside this section sat an independent scholar named Dina who had mastered the art of reading ancient texts. Her belief was simple — honesty and proper study are the surfaces through which truth is found. One afternoon, she scanned the pages of a dusty book and noticed a different kind of writing on the surface of the back cover. She demonstrated to her student how to read it section by section. The idiom written there said 'Look ahead, not above.' It implied that evidence of knowledge is not in the stars — it is inside us. Dina explained that a common mistake people make is believing that wisdom costs a large amount of money or time. In reality, the proper amount of focus and a good memory are enough. Within that single afternoon, her student discovered evidence that the book was over three centuries old — a surface discovery that pointed to something far greater inside. Dina smiled and said, 'Now you are one step ahead. That is what independent learning feels like.'

opts: the top floor of the oldest library

comprehension.q: What did Dina demonstrate to her student?
opts: how to read the book section by section

comprehension.q: What idiom was written in the book?

opts: that the book was over three centuries old

listening.title: A Study Discussion
listening.script:
A: Did you scan the first section of the chapter?
B: Yes, but there is a large amount of content inside.
A: What is your belief about the best way to study?
B: Honesty first — admit what you don't know and master it properly.
A: That is a different approach from the common method.
B: True. Most students just scan the surface and miss the evidence.
A: Can you demonstrate the proper way?
B: Sure. Start from the center of each section and go ahead from there.
A: What about memory? How do you strengthen it?
B: Independent practice. Don't rely on others to remind you.
A: That sounds like an idiom — stand on your own two feet.
B: Exactly. And the cost of good memory is just time and focus.
A: Above all, stay ahead of your schedule.
B: That is the proper way to master any subject.

comprehension.q: What does B say about honesty in study?
opts: admit what you don't know and master it

comprehension.q: What is the cost of good memory according to B?

speaking.prompt: Describe your personal method of learning. Use words like memory, scan, section, proper, and belief.
speaking.example: My belief is that proper learning takes time. I scan each section of a chapter first to get a surface understanding. Then I go deeper inside. I use my memory to connect different ideas. Above all, I stay honest about what I do not know yet.
tips: Start with your core belief about learning
Describe how you scan or read a section
End with what makes your method different

conversation.scenario: Discussing how to improve at a skill

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: What skill are you trying to master right now?

steps.speaker: user
steps.prompt: Name the skill and give your belief about it.

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: What is the common mistake people make when learning it?

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: What is the proper and independent way to get ahead?

steps.speaker: user
steps.prompt: Suggest a proper method using 'section', 'scan', and 'memory'.
